visa_types.name: Family Members Of Eu Or Eea Nationals
visa_types.documents_required:
Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3XcdCQcAKAUARCAiRrFAZ/8af31b038781784b4278723572e0b84c/document-checklist-ouagadougou.pdf)  to download the checklist for the document  required for a Long Stay Visa 
                                                                      Mandatory documents for online  applications and applications submitted by the company in Denmark
- A print of the confirmation       that the application was submitted online (the confirmation has       information about case order ID)
- Valid original travel document
- Other required supporting       documents followed by rightfully translated versions according to the list       covering the specific application form
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:   Family Members Of Eu Or Eea Nationals]
Service Charges

[Table:   Family Members Of Eu Or Eea Nationals]
Please click here to check visa fee (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
Note: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
Please Note:
- The applicable VFS service fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
- The visa fee should only be paid online at the time of form filling and service fee needs to be paid by cash at the implant bank counter in the application center at Bamako.
- The service fee is inclusive VAT and is non-refundable and non -transferable.
- Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from Additional Services tab.
visa_types.processing_time:
Bona fide cases (Approved by the  Embassy) – normally 15 calendar days, for a fully completed application with  proper documentation which is handed in at the VFS Visa Application Centre.  Please hand in your application well in advance during peak season.
Cases for Danish Immigration Service  (DIS) – See the processing time in https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service) and add 3-5 working days of  processing time at the Embassy.
The processing period may be  extended up to a maximum of 30 calendar days in individual cases, notably if  further scrutiny of the application is needed or in cases of representation  where the authorities of the represented Schengen State are consulted.
Exceptionally, when additional  documentation is needed in specific cases, the period may be extended up to a  maximum of 60 calendar days.
Citizens of certain countries or  categories of certain citizens are subject to prior consultation in one or more  Schengen countries before a visa may be issued. The processing time in these  cases will usually be 10-12 days as a minimum. See list of these nationalities and categories of persons  (annex 16 to the Visa code Handbook). (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/da/Lovstof/Visumkodeks-mm_/Visumh%C3%A5ndbogen)
visa_types.forms:
APPLYVISA:
"NEW ONLINE VISA APPLICATION:  From the 01 July 2019, you should fill in your visa application and pay the  application fee online using https://applyvisa.um.dk/ (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage). After you have applied and paid the visa  fee online, you must print and sign the cover letter from ApplyVisa and hand in  supporting documents and supply biometrics at the Visa Application Centre”
ONLINE PAYMENT OF R/W FEE (new  link!):
“From 01 July 2019, The Ministry  of Foreign Affairs of Denmark including all Danish diplomatic missions are  introducing online payment of the embassy fee for residence or work permit. 
The embassy fee should be paid  online using this link https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/)
The receipt for the payment should  be submitted at the Danish Visa Application Centre (VFS) when you hand in your  passport, relevant documentation and supply biometrics. Please note that in R/W  permit cases there are three fees to be paid. 1) The fee to either The Danish  Immigration Service or The Danish Agency for International Recruitment and  Integration (SIRI), 2) the fee to the Danish embassy (using the link https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/)) and 3) the fee to the Danish Visa  Application Centre (VFS).”
visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa is solely for family  members of EU or EEA nationals. This visa is given for a travel period of more  than 90 days.
You may be entitled to an  accelerated visa procedure free of charge if you meet the following criteria: 
- you are a family member of an       European Union (EU) or European Economic Area (EEA) citizen; and
- that EU/EEA citizen is       travelling to or is residing in a member state other than that of which       he/she is a citizen; and
- You are accompanying the EU/EEA       citizen or planning to join him/her in the Schengen State of destination.
If you think you are entitled to an  accelerated visa procedure you will need to show proof that you meet these  criteria when you submit your visa application.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This visa is solely for family  members of EU or EEA nationals. This visa is given for a travel period of more  than 90 days.
You may be entitled to an  accelerated visa procedure free of charge if you meet the following criteria: 
- you are a family member of an       European Union (EU) or European Economic Area (EEA) citizen; and
- that EU/EEA citizen is       travelling to or is residing in a member state other than that of which       he/she is a citizen; and
- You are accompanying the EU/EEA       citizen or planning to join him/her in the Schengen State of destination.
If you think you are entitled to an  accelerated visa procedure you will need to show proof that you meet these  criteria when you submit your visa application.

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Family Members Of Eu Or Eea Nationals]
Service Charges

[Table:   Family Members Of Eu Or Eea Nationals]
Please click here to check visa fee (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
Note: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
Please Note:
- The applicable VFS service fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
- The visa fee should only be paid online at the time of form filling and service fee needs to be paid by cash at the implant bank counter in the application center at Bamako.
- The service fee is inclusive VAT and is non-refundable and non -transferable.
- Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from Additional Services tab.

## Documents Required
Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3XcdCQcAKAUARCAiRrFAZ/8af31b038781784b4278723572e0b84c/document-checklist-ouagadougou.pdf)  to download the checklist for the document  required for a Long Stay Visa 
                                                                      Mandatory documents for online  applications and applications submitted by the company in Denmark
- A print of the confirmation       that the application was submitted online (the confirmation has       information about case order ID)
- Valid original travel document
- Other required supporting       documents followed by rightfully translated versions according to the list       covering the specific application form

## Photo Specifications
Size  of photograph for visa applications
After the instalment of a new  system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa application, it  is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm. Length of the head  should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of hair.
The photograph must still be in  color with bright background.
Danish Visa Application Centre has  been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa application  is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above format.
Applicants may therefore obtain a  new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa Application  Centre can receive the application.
For further information see the  print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit  website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)

## Processing Time
Bona fide cases (Approved by the  Embassy) – normally 15 calendar days, for a fully completed application with  proper documentation which is handed in at the VFS Visa Application Centre.  Please hand in your application well in advance during peak season.
Cases for Danish Immigration Service  (DIS) – See the processing time in https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service) and add 3-5 working days of  processing time at the Embassy.
The processing period may be  extended up to a maximum of 30 calendar days in individual cases, notably if  further scrutiny of the application is needed or in cases of representation  where the authorities of the represented Schengen State are consulted.
Exceptionally, when additional  documentation is needed in specific cases, the period may be extended up to a  maximum of 60 calendar days.
Citizens of certain countries or  categories of certain citizens are subject to prior consultation in one or more  Schengen countries before a visa may be issued. The processing time in these  cases will usually be 10-12 days as a minimum. See list of these nationalities and categories of persons  (annex 16 to the Visa code Handbook). (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/da/Lovstof/Visumkodeks-mm_/Visumh%C3%A5ndbogen)

## Download Forms
APPLYVISA:
"NEW ONLINE VISA APPLICATION:  From the 01 July 2019, you should fill in your visa application and pay the  application fee online using https://applyvisa.um.dk/ (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage). After you have applied and paid the visa  fee online, you must print and sign the cover letter from ApplyVisa and hand in  supporting documents and supply biometrics at the Visa Application Centre”
ONLINE PAYMENT OF R/W FEE (new  link!):
“From 01 July 2019, The Ministry  of Foreign Affairs of Denmark including all Danish diplomatic missions are  introducing online payment of the embassy fee for residence or work permit. 
The embassy fee should be paid  online using this link https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/)
The receipt for the payment should  be submitted at the Danish Visa Application Centre (VFS) when you hand in your  passport, relevant documentation and supply biometrics. Please note that in R/W  permit cases there are three fees to be paid. 1) The fee to either The Danish  Immigration Service or The Danish Agency for International Recruitment and  Integration (SIRI), 2) the fee to the Danish embassy (using the link https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/)) and 3) the fee to the Danish Visa  Application Centre (VFS).”

visa_types.name: Business
visa_types.documents_required:
Please print out  this Checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/59f5jcH6Q4aVzAXP0bfrWH/0bd23e2aad15df690cbcac96c3c061ce/business-checklist-new.pdf) and prepare the required documents accordingly,  also prepare an additional copy of your Passport ID page for Passport  Collection.
Note:
- Kindly make       sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate       information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned       trip.
- In case any       document is missing on submission date, additional documents can be       submitted only by applicants themselves at VFS within 5 business days.
- Applicant can be  asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed  necessary by the Embassy.
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:   Business]
Service Charges

[Table:   Business]
Please click here to check visa fee (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
Note: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
Please Note:
The applicable VFS service fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
The visa fee should only be paid online at the time of form filling
The service fee is inclusive VAT and is non-refundable and non -transferable.
Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from Additional Services tab.
The service fee and additional services at The Visa Application Centre are to be paid only in FCFA.
The following categories of applicants are exempted of Visa fees only:
Spouse and Dependent Children of EU Nationals (Proof of EU Nationality and relationship with applicants needs to be provided). This is subject to EU treaty rights i.e. Spouse and Dependents cannot exercise this exemption when visiting country of origin of the EU National.
Children under 6.
The following are also exempt but would require to get confirmation from the Embassy directly before submission.
School pupils, students, post graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training.
Researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research.
Visa fee needs to be paid online and service fee needs to be paid in the application center in Mali.
The applicable visa fee in FCFA is as per current exchange rate approved by the Embassy. It is subject to change without notice.
The visa fee and service charges are non- refundable and non -transferable.
visa_types.processing_time:
The Danish mission has 15 days to make a decision on your  visa application from the date the mission receives your passport, application  and biometrics. In some cases, the processing time can be extended to 45 days  when further case processing is needed. 
            Cases for Danish Immigration  Service (DIS) – See the processing time inhttps://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service) and add 3-5 working days of  processing time at the Embassy.
Citizens of certain countries or  categories of certain citizens are subject to prior consultation in one or more  Schengen countries before a visa may be issued. The processing time in these  cases will usually be 10-12 days as a minimum. See list of these  nationalities and categories of persons (annex 16 to the Visa code Handbook). (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/da/Lovstof/Visumkodeks-mm_/Visumh%C3%A5ndbogen)
visa_types.forms:
Register your visa application online and  book an appointment with VFS 

                                                                    "NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA  APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and  pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa fee  online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) .
Click  here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form 

                                                                    After completing the visa  application form and paying visa fee online, you must book an appointment to  submit your documents at VFS by clicking here. Make sure to bring along all the required documents (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7rxrziaeRLAw4Rw3E9DU7z/e38f8314324628e73d0a4cc777350f38/bussiness-visa-document.pdf) and hand in supporting documents at the  Visa Application Centre.

                                                                    Make sure you are on time for your  appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the  application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their  appointment. Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are  unable to make your appointment.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
The visa is issued to traders and businessmen/women or  highly skilled professionals (engineers, lawyers, medical doctors, and so on)  who wish to go to Denmark for reasons related to their profession. It's a  Schengen visa (type "C") - Maximum stay: 90
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The visa is issued to traders and businessmen/women or  highly skilled professionals (engineers, lawyers, medical doctors, and so on)  who wish to go to Denmark for reasons related to their profession. It's a  Schengen visa (type "C") - Maximum stay: 90

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Business]
Service Charges

[Table:   Business]
Please click here to check visa fee (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
Note: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
Please Note:
The applicable VFS service fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
The visa fee should only be paid online at the time of form filling
The service fee is inclusive VAT and is non-refundable and non -transferable.
Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from Additional Services tab.
The service fee and additional services at The Visa Application Centre are to be paid only in FCFA.
The following categories of applicants are exempted of Visa fees only:
Spouse and Dependent Children of EU Nationals (Proof of EU Nationality and relationship with applicants needs to be provided). This is subject to EU treaty rights i.e. Spouse and Dependents cannot exercise this exemption when visiting country of origin of the EU National.
Children under 6.
The following are also exempt but would require to get confirmation from the Embassy directly before submission.
School pupils, students, post graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training.
Researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research.
Visa fee needs to be paid online and service fee needs to be paid in the application center in Mali.
The applicable visa fee in FCFA is as per current exchange rate approved by the Embassy. It is subject to change without notice.
The visa fee and service charges are non- refundable and non -transferable.

## Documents Required
Please print out  this Checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/59f5jcH6Q4aVzAXP0bfrWH/0bd23e2aad15df690cbcac96c3c061ce/business-checklist-new.pdf) and prepare the required documents accordingly,  also prepare an additional copy of your Passport ID page for Passport  Collection.
Note:
- Kindly make       sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate       information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned       trip.
- In case any       document is missing on submission date, additional documents can be       submitted only by applicants themselves at VFS within 5 business days.
- Applicant can be  asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed  necessary by the Embassy.

## Photo Specifications
Size  of photograph for visa applications
After the instalment of a new  system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa application, it  is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm. Length of the head  should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of hair.
The photograph must still be in  color with bright background.
Danish Visa Application Centre has  been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa application  is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above format.
Applicants may therefore obtain a  new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa Application  Centre can receive the application.
For further information see the  print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit  website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)

## Processing Time
The Danish mission has 15 days to make a decision on your  visa application from the date the mission receives your passport, application  and biometrics. In some cases, the processing time can be extended to 45 days  when further case processing is needed. 
            Cases for Danish Immigration  Service (DIS) – See the processing time inhttps://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service) and add 3-5 working days of  processing time at the Embassy.
Citizens of certain countries or  categories of certain citizens are subject to prior consultation in one or more  Schengen countries before a visa may be issued. The processing time in these  cases will usually be 10-12 days as a minimum. See list of these  nationalities and categories of persons (annex 16 to the Visa code Handbook). (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/da/Lovstof/Visumkodeks-mm_/Visumh%C3%A5ndbogen)

## Download Forms

                                                                    Register your visa application online and  book an appointment with VFS 

                                                                    "NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA  APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and  pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa fee  online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) .
Click  here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form 

                                                                    After completing the visa  application form and paying visa fee online, you must book an appointment to  submit your documents at VFS by clicking here. Make sure to bring along all the required documents (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7rxrziaeRLAw4Rw3E9DU7z/e38f8314324628e73d0a4cc777350f38/bussiness-visa-document.pdf) and hand in supporting documents at the  Visa Application Centre.

                                                                    Make sure you are on time for your  appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the  application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their  appointment. Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are  unable to make your appointment.

Overview
This visa is solely for family members of Non-EU or  EEA nationals. This visa is given for a travel period of more than 90 days.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This visa is solely for family members of Non-EU or  EEA nationals. This visa is given for a travel period of more than 90 days.

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Family Reunification With Non- Eu Citizen]
Service Charges

[Table:   Family Reunification With Non- Eu Citizen]

Cases for Danish Immigration Service  (DIS) – See the processing time in https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service) and add 3-5 working days of  processing time at the Embassy.
The processing period may be  extended up to a maximum of 30 calendar days in individual cases, notably if  further scrutiny of the application is needed or in cases of representation  where the authorities of the represented Schengen State are consulted.
Exceptionally, when additional  documentation is needed in specific cases, the period may be extended up to a  maximum of 60 calendar days.
Citizens of certain countries or  categories of certain citizens are subject to prior consultation in one or more  Schengen countries before a visa may be issued. The processing time in these  cases will usually be 10-12 days as a minimum. See list of these nationalities and categories of persons  (annex 16 to the Visa code Handbook). (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/da/Lovstof/Visumkodeks-mm_/Visumh%C3%A5ndbogen)

## Download Forms
APPLYVISA:
"NEW ONLINE VISA APPLICATION:  From the 01 July 2019, you should fill in your visa application and pay the  application fee online using https://applyvisa.um.dk/ (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage). After you have applied and paid the visa  fee online, you must print and sign the cover letter from ApplyVisa and hand in  supporting documents and supply biometrics at the Visa Application Centre”
ONLINE PAYMENT OF R/W FEE (new  link!):
“From 01 July 2019, The Ministry  of Foreign Affairs of Denmark including all Danish diplomatic missions are  introducing online payment of the embassy fee for residence or work permit. 
The embassy fee should be paid  online using this link https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/)
The receipt for the payment should  be submitted at the Danish Visa Application Centre (VFS) when you hand in your  passport, relevant documentation and supply biometrics. Please note that in R/W  permit cases there are three fees to be paid. 1) The fee to either The Danish  Immigration Service or The Danish Agency for International Recruitment and  Integration (SIRI), 2) the fee to the Danish embassy (using the link https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/)) and 3) the fee to the Danish Visa  Application Centre (VFS).”

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A residence permit is given only upon approval by Danish Immigration Service in Copenhagen – the process may take 4 to 6 months. Application forms can be downloaded (Please note that each of the application forms for Danish residence and work permits has been developed for a specific type of applicant).

Further information can be found on the website of Danish Immigration Service: www.newtodenmark.dk (http://www.newtodenmark.dk/) www.newtodenmark.dk (http://www.newtodenmark.dk)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A residence permit is given only upon approval by Danish Immigration Service in Copenhagen – the process may take 4 to 6 months. Application forms can be downloaded (Please note that each of the application forms for Danish residence and work permits has been developed for a specific type of applicant).

Further information can be found on the website of Danish Immigration Service: www.newtodenmark.dk (http://www.newtodenmark.dk/) www.newtodenmark.dk (http://www.newtodenmark.dk)

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Work And Residence Permit]
Service Charges

[Table:   Work And Residence Permit]
Please click here to check visa fee (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
Note: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
Please Note:
- The applicable VFS service fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
- The visa fee should only be paid online at the time of form filling and service fee needs to be paid by cash at the implant bank counter in the application center at Bamako.
- The service fee is inclusive VAT and is non-refundable and non -transferable.
- Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from Additional Services tab.

## Documents Required
 Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3XcdCQcAKAUARCAiRrFAZ/8af31b038781784b4278723572e0b84c/document-checklist-ouagadougou.pdf) to download the checklist for the document  required for a Long Stay Visa 

                                                                        Mandatory documents for online  applications and applications submitted by the company in Denmark
- A print of the confirmation       that the application was submitted online (the confirmation has       information about case order ID)
- Valid original travel document
- Other required supporting       documents followed by rightfully translated versions according to the list       covering the specific application form

## Photo Specifications
Size  of photograph for visa applications
After the instalment of a new  system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa application, it  is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm. Length of the head  should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of hair.
The photograph must still be in  color with bright background.
Danish Visa Application Centre has  been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa application  is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above format.
Applicants may therefore obtain a  new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa Application  Centre can receive the application.
For further information see the  print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit  website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)

## Processing Time
A residence permit is given only  upon approval by Danish Immigration Service in Copenhagen – the process may  take 4 to 6 months. Such a permit is valid for Denmark only, not the entire Schengen-area.  Application forms can be downloaded (Please note that each of the application  forms for Danish residence and work permits has been developed for a specific  type of applicant).
Further information can be found on the  website of Danish Immigration Service: www.newtodenmark.dk (https://www.newtodenmark.dk)

## Download Forms
APPLYVISA:
"NEW ONLINE VISA APPLICATION:  From the 01 July 2019, you should fill in your visa application and pay the  application fee online using https://applyvisa.um.dk/ (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage). After you have applied and paid the visa  fee online, you must print and sign the cover letter from ApplyVisa and hand in  supporting documents and supply biometrics at the Visa Application Centre”
ONLINE PAYMENT OF R/W FEE (new  link!):
“From 01 July 2019, The Ministry  of Foreign Affairs of Denmark including all Danish diplomatic missions are  introducing online payment of the embassy fee for residence or work permit. 
The embassy fee should be paid  online using this link https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/)
The receipt for the payment should  be submitted at the Danish Visa Application Centre (VFS) when you hand in your  passport, relevant documentation and supply biometrics. Please note that in R/W  permit cases there are three fees to be paid. 1) The fee to either The Danish  Immigration Service or The Danish Agency for International Recruitment and  Integration (SIRI), 2) the fee to the Danish embassy (using the link https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/)) and 3) the fee to the Danish Visa  Application Centre (VFS).”

visa_types.name: Visiting Family And Friends
visa_types.documents_required:
Please print out this Checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4AF43uURWgzsDG4Ys7DqKi/967905e04a0f53d0d61671083edab0d7/visit-checklist-new.pdf) and prepare the required documents accordingly,  also prepare an additional copy of your Passport ID page for Passport  Collection.
Note:
- Kindly make sure that your application       contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information       must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- In case any document is missing on       submission date, additional documents can be submitted only by applicants       themselves at VFS within 5 business days.
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents  or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:   Visiting Family And Friends]
Service Charges

[Table:   Visiting Family And Friends]
Please click here to check visa fee (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
Note: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
Please Note:
The applicable VFS service fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
The visa fee should only be paid online at the time of form filling
The service fee is inclusive VAT and is non-refundable and non -transferable.
Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from Additional Services tab.
The service fee and additional services at The Visa Application Centre are to be paid only in FCFA.
The following categories of applicants are exempted of Visa fees only:
Spouse and Dependent Children of EU Nationals (Proof of EU Nationality and relationship with applicants needs to be provided). This is subject to EU treaty rights i.e. Spouse and Dependents cannot exercise this exemption when visiting country of origin of the EU National.
Children under 6.
The following are also exempt but would require to get confirmation from the Embassy directly before submission.
School pupils, students, post graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training.
Researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research.
Visa fee needs to be paid online and service fee needs to be paid in the application center in Mali.
The applicable visa fee in FCFA is as per current exchange rate approved by the Embassy. It is subject to change without notice.
The visa fee and service charges are non- refundable and non -transferable.
visa_types.processing_time:
The Danish mission has 15 days to make a decision on your  visa application from the date the mission receives your passport, application  and biometrics. In some cases, the processing time can be extended to 45 days  when further case processing is needed. 
            Cases for Danish Immigration  Service (DIS) – See the processing time inhttps://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service) and add 3-5 working days of  processing time at the Embassy.
Citizens of certain countries or  categories of certain citizens are subject to prior consultation in one or more  Schengen countries before a visa may be issued. The processing time in these  cases will usually be 10-12 days as a minimum. See list of these  nationalities and categories of persons (annex 16 to the Visa code Handbook). (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/da/Lovstof/Visumkodeks-mm_/Visumh%C3%A5ndbogen)
visa_types.forms:
Register your visa application online and  book an appointment with VFS 
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA  APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and  pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa fee  online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)  .
Click  here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)  to fill in your visa application  form 
After completing the visa  application form and paying visa fee online, you must book an appointment to  submit your documents at VFS by clicking here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/bfa/en/dnk/book-an-appointment) . Make sure to bring along all the required documents (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7rxrziaeRLAw4Rw3E9DU7z/e38f8314324628e73d0a4cc777350f38/bussiness-visa-document.pdf)  and hand in supporting documents at  the Visa Application Centre.
Make sure you are  on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee  that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule  their appointment. Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you  are unable to make your appointment.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa is solely for the purpose of  Visiting Family and Friends. This visa is given for a travel period of less  than 90 days.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This visa is solely for the purpose of  Visiting Family and Friends. This visa is given for a travel period of less  than 90 days. 

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Visiting Family And Friends]
Service Charges

[Table:   Visiting Family And Friends]
Please click here to check visa fee (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
Note: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
Please Note:
The applicable VFS service fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
The visa fee should only be paid online at the time of form filling
The service fee is inclusive VAT and is non-refundable and non -transferable.
Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from Additional Services tab.
The service fee and additional services at The Visa Application Centre are to be paid only in FCFA.
The following categories of applicants are exempted of Visa fees only:
Spouse and Dependent Children of EU Nationals (Proof of EU Nationality and relationship with applicants needs to be provided). This is subject to EU treaty rights i.e. Spouse and Dependents cannot exercise this exemption when visiting country of origin of the EU National.
Children under 6.
The following are also exempt but would require to get confirmation from the Embassy directly before submission.
School pupils, students, post graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training.
Researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research.
Visa fee needs to be paid online and service fee needs to be paid in the application center in Mali.
The applicable visa fee in FCFA is as per current exchange rate approved by the Embassy. It is subject to change without notice.
The visa fee and service charges are non- refundable and non -transferable.

## Documents Required
Please print out this Checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4AF43uURWgzsDG4Ys7DqKi/967905e04a0f53d0d61671083edab0d7/visit-checklist-new.pdf) and prepare the required documents accordingly,  also prepare an additional copy of your Passport ID page for Passport  Collection.
Note:
- Kindly make sure that your application       contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information       must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- In case any document is missing on       submission date, additional documents can be submitted only by applicants       themselves at VFS within 5 business days.
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents  or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.

## Photo Specifications
Size  of photograph for visa applications
After the instalment of a new  system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa application, it  is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm. Length of the head  should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of hair.
The photograph must still be in  color with bright background.
Danish Visa Application Centre has  been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa application  is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above format.
Applicants may therefore obtain a  new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa Application  Centre can receive the application.
For further information see the  print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit  website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)

## Processing Time
The Danish mission has 15 days to make a decision on your  visa application from the date the mission receives your passport, application  and biometrics. In some cases, the processing time can be extended to 45 days  when further case processing is needed. 
            Cases for Danish Immigration  Service (DIS) – See the processing time inhttps://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service) and add 3-5 working days of  processing time at the Embassy.
Citizens of certain countries or  categories of certain citizens are subject to prior consultation in one or more  Schengen countries before a visa may be issued. The processing time in these  cases will usually be 10-12 days as a minimum. See list of these  nationalities and categories of persons (annex 16 to the Visa code Handbook). (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/da/Lovstof/Visumkodeks-mm_/Visumh%C3%A5ndbogen)

## Download Forms
Register your visa application online and  book an appointment with VFS 
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA  APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and  pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa fee  online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)  .
Click  here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)  to fill in your visa application  form 
After completing the visa  application form and paying visa fee online, you must book an appointment to  submit your documents at VFS by clicking here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/bfa/en/dnk/book-an-appointment) . Make sure to bring along all the required documents (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7rxrziaeRLAw4Rw3E9DU7z/e38f8314324628e73d0a4cc777350f38/bussiness-visa-document.pdf)  and hand in supporting documents at  the Visa Application Centre.
Make sure you are  on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee  that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule  their appointment. Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you  are unable to make your appointment.

The photograph must still be in  color with bright background.
Danish Visa Application Centre has  been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa application  is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above format.
Applicants may therefore obtain a  new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa Application  Centre can receive the application.
For further information see the  print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit  website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)

## Processing Time
The Danish mission has 15 days to make a decision on your  visa application from the date the mission receives your passport, application  and biometrics. In some cases, the processing time can be extended to 45 days  when further case processing is needed. 
            Cases for Danish Immigration  Service (DIS) – See the processing time inhttps://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service) and add 3-5 working days of  processing time at the Embassy.
Citizens of certain countries or  categories of certain citizens are subject to prior consultation in one or more  Schengen countries before a visa may be issued. The processing time in these  cases will usually be 10-12 days as a minimum. See list of these  nationalities and categories of persons (annex 16 to the Visa code Handbook). (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/da/Lovstof/Visumkodeks-mm_/Visumh%C3%A5ndbogen)

## Download Forms
Register your visa application online and  book an appointment with VFS 
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA  APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and  pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa fee  online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)  .
Click  here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)  to fill in your visa application  form 
After completing the visa  application form and paying visa fee online, you must book an appointment to  submit your documents at VFS by clicking here . Make sure to bring along all the required documents (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7rxrziaeRLAw4Rw3E9DU7z/e38f8314324628e73d0a4cc777350f38/bussiness-visa-document.pdf)  and hand in supporting documents at  the Visa Application Centre.
Make sure you are on time for your appointment;  for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be  submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment. Kindly,  notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are unable to make your  appointment.

visa_types.name: Tourist
visa_types.documents_required:
Please print out  this Checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/54jfcST4I1UWq4yZc8a54y/4add429ec5d19de9243b568c1fe599ff/tourist-visa-checklist-french-FINAL.pdf) and prepare the required documents accordingly,  also prepare an additional copy of your Passport ID page for Passport  Collection. 
Note: 
- Kindly make       sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate       information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned       trip.
- In case any       document is missing on submission date, additional documents can be       submitted only by applicants themselves at VFS within 5 business days.
- Applicant can be  asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed  necessary by the Embassy.
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:   Tourist]
Service Charges

[Table:   Tourist]
Please click here to check visa fee (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
Note: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
Please Note:
The applicable VFS service fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
The visa fee should only be paid online at the time of form filling
The service fee is inclusive VAT and is non-refundable and non -transferable.
Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from Additional Services tab.
The service fee and additional services at The Visa Application Centre are to be paid only in FCFA.
The following categories of applicants are exempted of Visa fees only:
Spouse and Dependent Children of EU Nationals (Proof of EU Nationality and relationship with applicants needs to be provided). This is subject to EU treaty rights i.e. Spouse and Dependents cannot exercise this exemption when visiting country of origin of the EU National.
Children under 6.
The following are also exempt but would require to get confirmation from the Embassy directly before submission.
School pupils, students, post graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training.
Researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research.
Visa fee needs to be paid online and service fee needs to be paid in the application center in Mali.
The applicable visa fee in FCFA is as per current exchange rate approved by the Embassy. It is subject to change without notice.
The visa fee and service charges are non- refundable and non -transferable.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This  visa is solely for the purpose of leisure travel including vacationing,  cruising, sightseeing and other recreational activities. This visa is given for  a travel period of less than 90 days.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This  visa is solely for the purpose of leisure travel including vacationing,  cruising, sightseeing and other recreational activities. This visa is given for  a travel period of less than 90 days. 

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Tourist]
Service Charges

[Table:   Tourist]
Please click here to check visa fee (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
Note: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
Please Note:
The applicable VFS service fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
The visa fee should only be paid online at the time of form filling
The service fee is inclusive VAT and is non-refundable and non -transferable.
Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from Additional Services tab.
The service fee and additional services at The Visa Application Centre are to be paid only in FCFA.
The following categories of applicants are exempted of Visa fees only:
Spouse and Dependent Children of EU Nationals (Proof of EU Nationality and relationship with applicants needs to be provided). This is subject to EU treaty rights i.e. Spouse and Dependents cannot exercise this exemption when visiting country of origin of the EU National.
Children under 6.
The following are also exempt but would require to get confirmation from the Embassy directly before submission.
School pupils, students, post graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training.
Researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research.
Visa fee needs to be paid online and service fee needs to be paid in the application center in Mali.
The applicable visa fee in FCFA is as per current exchange rate approved by the Embassy. It is subject to change without notice.
The visa fee and service charges are non- refundable and non -transferable.

## Documents Required
Please print out  this Checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/54jfcST4I1UWq4yZc8a54y/4add429ec5d19de9243b568c1fe599ff/tourist-visa-checklist-french-FINAL.pdf) and prepare the required documents accordingly,  also prepare an additional copy of your Passport ID page for Passport  Collection. 
Note: 
- Kindly make       sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate       information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned       trip.
- In case any       document is missing on submission date, additional documents can be       submitted only by applicants themselves at VFS within 5 business days.
- Applicant can be  asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed  necessary by the Embassy.

## Photo Specifications
After the instalment of a new  system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa application, it  is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm. Length of the head  should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of hair.
The photograph must still be in  color with bright background.
Danish Visa Application Centre has  been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa application  is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above format.
Applicants may therefore obtain a  new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa Application  Centre can receive the application.
For further information see the  print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit  website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)

## Processing Time
The Danish mission has 15 days to make a decision on your  visa application from the date the mission receives your passport, application  and biometrics. In some cases, the processing time can be extended to 45 days  when further case processing is needed. 
            Cases for Danish Immigration  Service (DIS) – See the processing time inhttps://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service) and add 3-5 working days of  processing time at the Embassy.
Citizens of certain countries or  categories of certain citizens are subject to prior consultation in one or more  Schengen countries before a visa may be issued. The processing time in these  cases will usually be 10-12 days as a minimum. See list of these  nationalities and categories of persons (annex 16 to the Visa code Handbook). (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/da/Lovstof/Visumkodeks-mm_/Visumh%C3%A5ndbogen)

## Download Forms
Register  your visa application online and book an appointment with VFS
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA  APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and  pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa fee  online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) ‘’ .
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application  form 
After completing the visa  application form and paying visa fee online, you must book an appointment to  submit your documents at VFS by clicking here. Make sure to bring along all the required documents and hand in supporting documents at  the Visa Application Centre.
Make sure you are on time for your  appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the  application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their  appointment. Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are  unable to make your appointment.
